steps.title: Identify the key constraint
steps.content: The clause “no two minor planets are allowed to have the same name” directly establishes what property the chosen name must possess: it must be unique to each planet.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the answers
steps.content: ”Exclusive to” ensures each name belongs to only one body, which directly addresses the stated rule preventing name duplication. The other choices don't establish this same principle: they address what a name suggests, what's typical or characteristic, or are logically incoherent. That matches choice A — A is correct.
